title: myTax 2019 Bonuses from life insurance companies and friendly societies
description: How to report bonuses from life insurance companies and friendly societies in myTax.
keywords: 2019, life insurance bonuses, insurance companies, friendly societies, myTax, e-tax, online tax return, etax

content_blocks.text: You receive a bonus because your life insurance policy or friendly society insurance bond matured, or was terminated, forfeited, or surrendered in full or in part.
Life insurance policies are issued by life insurance companies and friendly societies.
You may need to include some or all of a bonus amount in your assessable income.
Essentials
You need to include a bonus amount at this section only if all of the following conditions apply:

items: within 10 years from the policy start or re-start date , you received the bonus amount, or directed how it is to be dealt with
your life insurance policy or friendly society insurance bond matured, or was terminated, forfeited, or surrendered in full or in part, and
the bonus amount was in addition to the premiums you paid to the life insurance company or friendly society.

content_blocks.text: You cannot claim a loss if your life insurance policy or friendly society insurance bond matured or was partly or fully surrendered, terminated or forfeited.
If you are not sure whether you need to include your bonus, phone 13 28 61 .
Do not show at this section
Do not declare as income:

items: any bonus amounts received after the 10th year of the policy

any life insurance bonuses from policies that you started before 28 August 1982 matured due to the death of the person insured you surrendered due to an accident, illness or other disability of the person insured you surrendered due to severe financial hardship are retirement savings accounts.

content_blocks.text: If you have received a statement detailing the bonus amount allocated to your continuing life insurance policy or friendly society bond but you have not actually received the bonus or directed how it is to be dealt with, do not include the bonus amount as income.
If you received any bonus amounts distributed from friendly society income bonds, see Other income .
Completing this section
You will need your:

items: life insurance policy and bonus advice
friendly society policy and bonus advice.
At Any other income, select Add for each bonus from life insurance policies and friendly society insurance bonds.

At Type of payment , select Bonuses from life insurance and friendly societies and enter information into the corresponding fields. From your bonus advice, enter the whole bonus amount you received in 2018–19 if you received it during the first eight years of the policy two-thirds of the bonus amount you received in 2018–19 if you received it during the ninth year of the policy, or one-third of the bonus amount you received in 2018–19 if you received it during the 10th year of the policy

content_blocks.text: Tax offset
If you show a bonus from life insurance or friendly societies, you will get a non-refundable tax offset equal to 30% of the bonus amount. We will work out this tax offset for you.
Policy start date
Your policy start date is usually written on the policy or bond. Refer to your policy, bond or provider if you are unsure of your policy start date.
Reset of the policy start date
If you increased your premiums by more than 25% of premiums paid in the prior policy year, your policy is taken to start at the beginning of the policy year in which you paid the higher premiums.
This means that you may need to include some or all of a bonus amount in your assessable income.

content_text:
You receive a bonus because your life insurance policy or friendly society insurance bond matured, or was terminated, forfeited, or surrendered in full or in part.
Life insurance policies are issued by life insurance companies and friendly societies.
You may need to include some or all of a bonus amount in your assessable income.
## Essentials
You need to include a bonus amount at this section only if all of the following conditions apply:
- within 10 years from the policy start or re-start date , you received the bonus amount, or directed how it is to be dealt with
- your life insurance policy or friendly society insurance bond matured, or was terminated, forfeited, or surrendered in full or in part, and
- the bonus amount was in addition to the premiums you paid to the life insurance company or friendly society.
You cannot claim a loss if your life insurance policy or friendly society insurance bond matured or was partly or fully surrendered, terminated or forfeited.
If you are not sure whether you need to include your bonus, phone 13 28 61 .
## Do not show at this section
Do not declare as income:
- any bonus amounts received after the 10th year of the policy
- any life insurance bonuses from policies that you started before 28 August 1982 matured due to the death of the person insured you surrendered due to an accident, illness or other disability of the person insured you surrendered due to severe financial hardship are retirement savings accounts.
If you have received a statement detailing the bonus amount allocated to your continuing life insurance policy or friendly society bond but you have not actually received the bonus or directed how it is to be dealt with, do not include the bonus amount as income.
If you received any bonus amounts distributed from friendly society income bonds, see Other income .
## Completing this section
You will need your:
- life insurance policy and bonus advice
- friendly society policy and bonus advice.
- At Any other income, select Add for each bonus from life insurance policies and friendly society insurance bonds.
- At Type of payment , select Bonuses from life insurance and friendly societies and enter information into the corresponding fields. From your bonus advice, enter the whole bonus amount you received in 2018–19 if you received it during the first eight years of the policy two-thirds of the bonus amount you received in 2018–19 if you received it during the ninth year of the policy, or one-third of the bonus amount you received in 2018–19 if you received it during the 10th year of the policy
- Select Save .
- Select Save and continue .
### Tax offset
If you show a bonus from life insurance or friendly societies, you will get a non-refundable tax offset equal to 30% of the bonus amount. We will work out this tax offset for you.
### Policy start date
Your policy start date is usually written on the policy or bond. Refer to your policy, bond or provider if you are unsure of your policy start date.
#### Reset of the policy start date
If you increased your premiums by more than 25% of premiums paid in the prior policy year, your policy is taken to start at the beginning of the policy year in which you paid the higher premiums.
This means that you may need to include some or all of a bonus amount in your assessable income.
